verbatim: Quick Upload creates a durable asynchronous batch.
action: Re-verify the durable-batch receipt end-to-end: POST a quick upload against the live backend, assert an ingest_batches row with stored_bytes>0 and item states persists in Mongo, restart the backend container, and assert the batch resumes/remains resumable (self-asserting script, non-zero exit on fail).
where: backend/services/ingestion/batches.py (batch creation ~:909-1088); Mongo ingest_batches / ingest_batch_items
why: uploads that survive restarts are the precondition for any document ever becoming retrievable evidence
feasibility_note: Ledger [x] with no recorded receipt. Code-level truth confirmed today: batches.py persists durable batch rows carrying stored_bytes (:909, :1045) and drop_off state (:806, :1038). What is missing is an e2e restart-survival receipt, which the ledger's own completion contract ('deployed + reconciled + verified') requires.
required_receipt: e2e script exits 0: batch id present in ingest_batches with stored_bytes>0 before AND after `docker compose restart backend`, and items reach a terminal stage afterward.

verbatim: Uploaded files are authoritative in the host-visible per-corpus drop-off.
action: Re-verify authority of the drop-off copy: upload a file, then assert (a) the file exists on the host volume ingest-drop-off/<corpus_id>/uploads/<batch_id>/ and (b) the ingest worker parses from that stored_path (drop_off_path recorded on the item), not from any request-scoped temp copy.
where: backend/services/ingestion/batches.py (:806 drop_off_path, :1020 stored_path, :1038 drop_off_dir); host volume ${POLYMATH_DOCKER_DATA_ROOT}/volumes/ingest-drop-off
why: a host-visible authoritative copy is what makes manual inspection, re-ingest, and the future watch-mode decision possible at all
feasibility_note: Ledger [x], receipt null. Code confirmed today: items persist drop_off_path (batches.py:806) and batches record drop_off_dir/drop_off_relative_dir (:1038-1039). 'Authoritative' (worker reads from the drop-off, nothing else) is asserted by docstring+flow but has no recorded e2e receipt.
required_receipt: Probe output showing the uploaded file at the host drop-off path and the parse job consuming that exact stored_path; script exit 0.

verbatim: Original filenames are preserved with deterministic collision suffixes.
action: Accept receipt: code verified 2026-07-13 — _unique_drop_off_path (batches.py:320-330) preserves the original name and appends deterministic ' (2)', ' (3)' suffixes with casefold collision detection against both reserved names and on-disk files. Optionally re-run the existing unit path in backend/tests/test_ingest_batches.py.
where: backend/services/ingestion/batches.py:314-330 (_safe_upload_filename, _unique_drop_off_path); backend/tests/test_ingest_batches.py
why: stable human-readable filenames keep the drop-off auditable and prevent silent overwrite of user documents
feasibility_note: Directly verified in source today: the function's docstring is literally 'Preserve the upload name, adding a deterministic suffix for duplicates' and the loop is deterministic (sequence=2,3,... with casefold reservation). This is the strongest-evidenced of the six [x] rows.
required_receipt: pytest backend/tests/test_ingest_batches.py -k upload exits 0 (covers stored filename behavior, e.g. stored_bytes assertion at :1440).

verbatim: Backend and ingest worker share the same drop-off mount.
action: Accept receipt: verified 2026-07-13 — backend (docker-compose.yml:475,477) and ingest worker (docker-compose.offline-ingest.yml:46-47) both mount ${POLYMATH_DOCKER_DATA_ROOT}/volumes/ingest-drop-off at /data/ingest-drop-off with identical INGEST_DROP_OFF_DIR defaults (compose :670 / offline :118). Re-check after any compose refactor.
where: docker-compose.yml:475-477,669-670; docker-compose.offline-ingest.yml:43-47,117-118
why: without the shared mount the worker cannot see uploaded bytes and ingestion silently starves
feasibility_note: Config-level claim, config-level proof obtained today. Operational caveat from deploy history: the offline-ingest overlay deploy requires all three -f compose files + --profile, and dropping an overlay silently changes mounts — so this receipt must be re-checked per deploy, not treated as permanent.
required_receipt: grep -n 'ingest-drop-off' docker-compose.yml docker-compose.offline-ingest.yml shows the same host volume on both services; docker inspect of both running containers shows the same Mounts entry.

verbatim: Quota checks use Mongo `stored_bytes`; filesystem walk is fallback-only.
action: Accept receipt: verified 2026-07-13 — batches.py:283-306 aggregates Mongo ingest_batches.stored_bytes on the request path and falls back to _directory_size_bytes(storage_root) only inside the except branch ('compatibility fallback for older/fake databases').
where: backend/services/ingestion/batches.py:280-306; Mongo ingest_batches.stored_bytes
why: an indexed byte ledger keeps quota checks O(1) on the upload hot path instead of walking the whole store
feasibility_note: Source verified today; the docstring states the exact claim. Silent-fallback-accounting rule applies: the except-branch walk is a silent fallback with no counter — if the aggregation ever starts failing, quota checks degrade invisibly. Worth a one-line fallback counter, but that is an improvement, not a gap in this claim.
required_receipt: Cited lines batches.py:290-299 (aggregate on stored_bytes) and :298 (walk only in except); optionally a unit test forcing the exception path to prove the fallback still works.

verbatim: UI reports the relative drop-off path.
action: Re-verify UI display end-to-end: upload via the real frontend at :3000 (localStorage polymath-auth injection) and assert the rendered batch view shows drop_off_relative_dir ('<corpus_id>/uploads/<batch_id>'), not an absolute container path.
where: backend/services/ingestion/batches.py:1039 (drop_off_relative_dir); frontend batch/upload view; e2e harness per live-trace-tabs.spec.ts pattern
why: a relative path is the only path a user can act on from the host, closing the loop on the filesystem contract
feasibility_note: API side verified today (batches.py:1039 computes and returns the relative dir). The 'UI reports' half has no receipt; note the frontend container serves a build-time dist, so any UI assertion must run against the deployed build, not source.
required_receipt: Frontend e2e spec exits 0 asserting the relative drop-off path string is rendered after an upload.

verbatim: Decide whether manually placed files should be watched automatically or require explicit Folder Sync.
action: Put the watch-mode question to the owner with a one-page option sheet: (A) explicit Folder Sync only (no watcher; manually placed files inert until synced) vs (B) automatic watcher with the L1195 state machine. Record the decision and its date in the ledger's Quick Upload section.
who: owner (genuine product decision); junior-agent drafts the option sheet
where: /Users/king/polymath_v3.3/docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md (Quick Upload And Filesystem Contract section); no code until decided
why: decides whether the drop-off is a passive archive or an active ingestion surface, which changes duplicate-protection risk for retrieval
feasibility_note: No dedicated S-slot exists; the plan-of-record table (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 1: 'Quick upload contract ... Watch-mode decision') and session task #16 group it with the final-gate lane. The decision itself is cheap and can be taken any time; it blocks only GTE:L1195. Option A is the zero-build default consistent with the 'pressure gates active during backfills' invariant (a watcher is a new uncontrolled ingestion trigger).
required_receipt: Ledger edit recording the decision (watch vs explicit-sync-only) with owner sign-off date; if A, L1195 is closed as superseded-by-decision.

verbatim: If watch mode is added, implement inbox/processing/processed/dead-letter states with durable identity and duplicate protection.
action: HOLD until the L1193 decision. If watch mode is adopted: build inbox/processing/processed/dead-letter directory states keyed by durable item identity (content hash + original name), reusing the existing durable-job/lease machinery (P0.6 job_leases pattern) for duplicate protection and idempotent replay; land with an asserting integration test that replays the same file twice and proves exactly-once ingestion.
who: backend ingestion service (worker + batches); junior-agent for the replay test
where: backend/services/ingestion/batches.py, backend/services/ingestion/worker.py, drop-off volume layout; new state-machine module
why: an unwatched inbox with no dead-letter lane turns manual file drops into silent ingestion loss or duplicates
feasibility_note: Conditional on GTE:L1193 choosing watch mode. Substrate exists: durable identity (item ids + stored_bytes ledger), duplicate suffix logic (batches.py:320), and lease/idempotent-replay machinery (P0.6, 11 [x]). The invariant 'pressure gates, durable leases, idempotent replay remain active during all backfills' must extend to watcher-triggered ingestion.
required_receipt: Integration test: drop the same file into inbox twice → exactly one document ingested, second copy lands in processed-as-duplicate; corrupt file → dead-letter with durable reason; exit 0.

verbatim: Run every row for Fast, Hybrid, and Graph unless the row explicitly tests a tier-specific feature.
action: Encode the rule into the matrix runner itself: the S14 harness iterates every query row across RetrievalTier.qdrant_only / qdrant_mongo / qdrant_mongo_graph and refuses to emit a report if any (row, tier) cell is missing without an explicit tier-specific-feature exemption flag on the row spec.
who: junior-agent (matrix runner) on the three_tier_eval/run_heldout_eval substrate
where: backend/services/retriever/three_tier_eval.py (ROUTES :25-35), backend/scripts/run_heldout_eval.py, new matrix row spec file
why: tier-conditional bugs (e.g. graph-only regressions) are invisible unless every row runs on every tier
feasibility_note: Terminology: 'Fast, Hybrid, Graph' are the informal names; canonical enum is RetrievalTier {qdrant_only, qdrant_mongo, qdrant_mongo_graph} (registry_retrieval 'tier' row — the bare word 'tier' collides with source_tier, Tier-0 routing, and tier_chunker Tiers A/B/C, so the runner must use the enum). This duplicates the first Non-Negotiable Invariant in matrix-scoped form; harness (three_tier_eval ROUTES) already enumerates all three.
required_receipt: Matrix report artifact whose schema is (row x 3 tiers) complete, with any skipped cell carrying an explicit tier_specific_exemption reason; runner exits non-zero on silent gaps.

verbatim: Expert term direct hit, such as FACS or Laban.
action: Author the matrix row: expert-term direct-hit query (FACS, Laban) with expected_doc_ids ground truth; run on all three RetrievalTier values; assert the expert term's documents are seated and cited.
where: backend/evals/heldout_questions.jsonl (shape 'direct' precedent) or new matrix spec; run via backend/scripts/run_heldout_eval.py
why: expert-vocabulary precision is the floor: if exact terminology cannot be retrieved, nothing else matters
feasibility_note: Heldout suite already contains 10 'direct'-shape questions (registry_eval), and FACS/Laban vocabulary lives in polymath_v2 (frozen but fully queryable). Registry_eval collision warning applies to ALL matrix rows: heldout_hashes.json is frozen at 58 questions while the 2026-07-13 EVAL baselines have n=56 — the matrix must pin question ids or re-baseline before comparing.
required_receipt: Matrix cell records for this row on all 3 tiers with doc_hit=true against pinned expected_doc_ids.

verbatim: Naive vocabulary bridge, such as “make the actor look less stiff.”
action: Author the matrix row: naive-phrasing query ('make the actor look less stiff') whose ground-truth docs use expert vocabulary; assert the vocabulary bridge (alias registry / latent-concept expansions) routes to them without drifting the protected original-query lane.
where: matrix spec; bridge substrate = S5 versioned alias registry + backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py
why: this row is the payoff test for the entire latent/alias program — lay users must reach expert content
feasibility_note: Run-order dependency: the bridge substrate does not exist yet on the PoC pair — latent_concepts coverage is ZERO on both pair corpora (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2, V) and latent aliases are currently read by nothing (C3). The row is only meaningful after S4 capture + S5 rollup (and S10 P2.2 for full lay-language reps). Running it earlier measures the wrong artifact — the exact C2 failure pattern.
required_receipt: Post-S5 matrix cell records showing the naive query seats the expert docs on all 3 tiers, with expansion provenance pointing at versioned registry entries (not CONCEPT_ALIASES Python dicts).

verbatim: Focused single-fact question.
action: Author the matrix row: focused single-fact question with one known answering chunk; assert the fact's parent/child is in final evidence and the answer cites it.
where: matrix spec; run_heldout_eval harness
why: single-fact precision guards against over-broad synthesis drowning the one right chunk
feasibility_note: No unbuilt dependency; the heldout suite and doc_hit scorer already support this shape. Cheapest row in the matrix.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with doc_hit=true and the answering chunk id in selected source IDs.

verbatim: Broad thematic synthesis.
action: Author the matrix row: broad thematic synthesis query; assert multi-document evidence with sane corpus/document distribution (no single-doc collapse) and grounded citations.
where: matrix spec; distribution fields from reservation/ranking diagnostics
why: synthesis breadth is where retrieval either demonstrates the library or parrots one book
feasibility_note: Runnable now; answer quality improves after S8 card rebuild + shelf_reserve A/B. Scoring is the soft part — define the pass rule (min distinct docs, no refusal) in advance per the hypothesis-promotion discipline (numeric targets set before the run).
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with >=N distinct docs in final evidence (N preregistered) and no unwarranted refusal.

verbatim: Enumeration/list requiring sibling expansion.
action: Author the matrix row: enumeration/list query whose complete answer requires sibling expansion (list items split across sibling parents); assert all list members are retrievable and cited.
where: matrix spec; sibling expansion = P1.6 answer-shape routing (S9)
why: lists split across chunk boundaries are the classic recall hole; sibling expansion is its only fix
feasibility_note: Dependency: sibling expansion is part of P1.6 (0 of 7 boxes, PLAN_CRITIQUE section 1) scheduled S9. Chunker history compounds it: lists were the worst structural gap of the blank-line chunker ('lists flattened'). Before S9 this row can only fail-and-baseline; schedule its pass-assertion after S9 lands.
required_receipt: Post-S9 3-tier cell records: enumeration query returns all ground-truth list members via sibling-expanded evidence, expansion visible in the run record.

verbatim: Procedure requiring ordered steps.
action: Author the matrix row: procedure query requiring ordered steps; assert steps appear in document order in evidence and the answer preserves ordering.
where: matrix spec; ordering depends on heading_path/section structure + P1.6 shape policy
why: procedures answered out of order are worse than refusals — they mislead the user
feasibility_note: Evidence-side substrate exists (heading_path non-empty 86-100% per corpus, PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2). The ordered-presentation half rides P1.6 answer-shape routing (S9); like L1207, assert-pass after S9.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with step chunks in correct section order in evidence text; ordering check scripted, not eyeballed.

verbatim: Comparison requiring evidence from multiple documents.
action: Author the matrix row: comparison query whose ground truth requires evidence from >=2 specific documents; assert both documents seat evidence in the final set.
where: matrix spec; distribution/reservation diagnostics (reservation_policy.py)
why: comparisons are the canonical multi-doc allocation test — one-sided evidence produces confidently wrong contrasts
feasibility_note: Substrate merged and live-probed: calibrated reservation_policy.py (P0.3, PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2). This row also operationalizes the historical per-side allocation problem (multi-doc chat formerly returning ~4/5 chunks from the title-matching doc).
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with expected_all_docs satisfied (evidence from each required doc) and reservation reasons naming why each side was seated.

verbatim: Follow-up anchored to a previously accepted document.
action: Author the matrix row: two-turn script — turn 1 establishes an accepted document, turn 2 asks a follow-up that must anchor to it; assert the follow-up seats the same document ahead of lexically-similar competitors.
who: junior-agent (matrix authoring + run, with history support in the harness)
where: matrix spec (heldout_questions.jsonl already supports optional history field); anchoring = P1.3 (S9)
why: conversation continuity is the difference between a librarian and a stateless search box
feasibility_note: Dependency: P1.3 conversation anchoring is 0 of 8 built (S9). Terminology (registry_retrieval): 'anchoring' here = conversation_anchoring (PLAN-ONLY, to-be-built) — do NOT confuse with the existing document_anchor title-recall lane, which already exists and would make a naive version of this row pass for the wrong reason. The row's ground truth must be a follow-up that document_anchor alone cannot satisfy.
required_receipt: Post-S9 3-tier cell records: turn-2 run record shows the anchor applied as a prior (inspectable), correct doc seated; a control run without history fails or ranks it lower.

verbatim: Negative control with nearby but non-answering vocabulary.
action: Author the matrix row: negative control with nearby-but-non-answering vocabulary; assert the system refuses (fail-closed) with a recorded refusal reason instead of confabulating from lexically-close chunks.
where: matrix spec; heldout shape negative_control; answerability gates (P0.4)
why: fail-closed on near-miss vocabulary is the non-negotiable honesty invariant of the whole system
feasibility_note: Substrate strong: P0.4 honest answerability is 7 [x] and negative_control is an existing heldout shape. Terminology (registry_eval): keep 'negative_control' (the heldout query shape) distinct from the 'forbidden-vocabulary control' implementation — this row is the former.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with answerable=false honored: refusal + refusal_reason populated, zero fabricated citations.

verbatim: Cross-domain within one corpus.
action: Author the matrix row: cross-domain query within one corpus (e.g. movement/film + AI topics inside polymath_v2); assert evidence spans both domains and neither domain's facet noise crowds out the other.
where: matrix spec; single-corpus multi-domain ground truth
why: within-corpus domain diversity tests ranking breadth without the corpus-boundary machinery
feasibility_note: Runnable now. Caveat: the legacy broad-alias facet family (agency_preservation on 89,088 parents, emotional_patterns on 38,492 — V) stamps 71-100% of every corpus and is DF-worthless as a domain discriminator; until the S7 owner decision lands, facet-aware ranking contributes noise to exactly this row. Pin the row's pass rule to doc-level ground truth, not facet distribution.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with ground-truth docs from both domains seated; re-run post-S7 to confirm facet cleanup did not regress it.

verbatim: Cross-corpus query requiring both corpora.
action: Author the matrix row: cross-corpus query (PoC pair) whose answer requires evidence from BOTH corpora; assert each necessary corpus retains evidence in the final set.
where: matrix spec; corpora = markbuildsbrands_transcripts + ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL; reservation_policy corpus floor
why: this is the direct acceptance for the corpus-floor program: multi-corpus questions must not collapse to one corpus
feasibility_note: This row IS the single remaining open P0.3 acceptance item ('cross-corpus retains evidence from each necessary corpus', PLAN_CRITIQUE section 1) — first provable at S8 when shelf_reserve wires through the calibrated reservation_policy, then re-run as a matrix row at S14. Substrate merged and live-probed.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records with final evidence containing >=1 chunk from each corpus and reservation reasons showing calibrated (not unconditional-floor) seating.

verbatim: Cross-corpus query where one selected corpus is irrelevant.
action: Author the matrix row: cross-corpus query where one selected corpus is irrelevant; assert the irrelevant corpus is NOT force-seated (no floor-driven junk) while the corpus boundary is still honored.
where: matrix spec; ranking_policy/reservation_policy relevance gating
why: the mirror of L1213: a floor that seats irrelevant corpora corrupts answers to satisfy a quota
feasibility_note: Directly exercises the Audit-Delta P0.3 gap ('relevance gating must cover already-selected candidates and the separate ranking_policy corpus-floor path'). The old unconditional per-corpus floor was REVERTED in favor of calibrated reservation — this row is the regression guard that it stays dead.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records: zero evidence seated from the irrelevant corpus purely for floor reasons; any seated chunk from it carries a relevance-passing score; user-selected corpus boundary untouched.

verbatim: Corpus with no tree but valid flat vectors.
action: Author the matrix row: query a fixture corpus that has valid flat child vectors but NO summary tree; assert graceful degradation (flat retrieval answers; no crash, no silent empty set) on all three tiers.
where: matrix spec; fixture corpus (clone or fresh mini-corpus with summary-tree build skipped); tree descent path in retriever
why: tree-optional behavior protects new/partially-ingested corpora from being unqueryable
feasibility_note: Needs deliberate fixture setup — all four live corpora currently have trees, so a no-tree corpus must be constructed (cheap: ingest a few docs with tree build disabled, or clone-and-drop summary_tree rows). Clone-first discipline applies; never mutate a live corpus to create the fixture.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records on the fixture: non-empty flat-vector evidence, run record explicitly notes tree route absent, no error.

verbatim: Deleted/non-queryable corpus.
action: Author the matrix row: query against a deleted/non-queryable corpus; assert a clean typed rejection (corpus not queryable) on all tiers — no partial answers from orphaned Qdrant collections.
where: matrix spec; authentic_library (deleted; historical Qdrant collections still exist per Current Durable Baseline); QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES gate
why: answering from a deleted corpus's orphan vectors is a data-integrity breach, not a retrieval win
feasibility_note: authentic_library is the natural live test subject — deleted with orphan Qdrant collections still present (baseline, L). Coordinate with P0.6: the scheduled orphan purge may remove the orphans before S14, in which case the row needs a fixture deleted-corpus instead. Registry_readiness note applies: 'queryable' must mean the QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES/corpus-status gate, not mere collection existence.
required_receipt: 3-tier cell records: request rejected with an explicit non-queryable reason; zero evidence returned from orphan collections.

verbatim: Cold-start query.
action: Author the matrix row: cold-start query — restart backend + sidecars, fire the first query with no warm caches; record latency and correctness and assert it completes within the preregistered cold-start budget.
where: matrix spec; embedder/reranker sidecars (:8082/:8081), vocabulary cache (P1.7), restart via docker compose + launchd kickstart
why: the first query after deploy is the one the owner always sees; cold-start pathology hides behind warm benchmarks
feasibility_note: Order dependency on S0: P1.8 embedder inference-warmup + status split is IN CODE unmerged, and P1.7 vocabulary cache awaits live verify (PLAN_CRITIQUE S0). Running this row before S0 lands measures the pre-warmup world and must be re-run after; the plan's deploy-train rule exists precisely to avoid such double measurement. Set the numeric budget in advance (hypothesis-promotion rule).
required_receipt: Cell records from a scripted restart→query run: correct answer + total time under the preregistered cold-start budget, with per-stage timings showing warmup state.

verbatim: Concurrent query while ingestion/backfill is active.
action: Author the matrix row: fire queries concurrently with an active ingestion/backfill (UGO canary ingest running); assert answers stay correct and latency stays within the preregistered degraded budget, with pressure gates visibly active.
where: matrix spec; UGO_CORPUS canary for the concurrent ingest; pressure-gate diagnostics
why: real users query during backfills; correctness under contention is a production property, not a benchmark one
feasibility_note: Fully orchestratable (canary corpus designated for exactly this). Hard physical constraint to encode in the budget: MLX embedder+reranker share ONE Metal GPU — measured contention takes p50 12s→31s and embed 0.4→8.6s when parallel loads collide. The pass threshold must be set with that reality, and the row must record whether ingest embedding was active during each query.
required_receipt: Cell records with concurrent-ingest flag=true, correct answers, latency within the preregistered contention budget, pressure-gate state captured in the run record.

verbatim: For every run record:
action: Encode the record contract as a typed run-record schema the matrix runner emits for EVERY (row, tier) cell; the runner fails the run if any of the eight field groups (L1222-L1229) is missing rather than silently omitting it.
where: matrix runner artifact schema (extend EVAL_*.json shape from run_heldout_eval.py); backend chat diagnostics (_schemas_legacy.py diagnostics fields)
why: an unrecorded run cannot be audited, compared, or regressed against — the record IS the deliverable
feasibility_note: Standing rule of the matrix, not a one-time box. The EVAL_*.json artifact convention and chat diagnostics (requested_tier/effective_tier etc.) give the skeleton; the eight groups below each have named substrate gaps critiqued per-row. Schema-validate the record (the repo's own Audit Delta 2 found zero Mongo validators — do not repeat that pattern in eval artifacts).
required_receipt: JSON-schema for the run record checked into backend/evals/, runner exits non-zero when a cell record fails validation.

verbatim: Plan, probes, obligations, and original-query lane.
action: Add to the run record: the retrieval plan, executed probes, coverage obligations, and explicit confirmation that the original-query protected lane ran unmodified (lane role 'original' present in fused pools).
where: backend/services/retriever/planned_fusion.py (lane roles), chat orchestrator plan/probe diagnostics; run-record schema
why: proves per-run that expansions never replaced the user's actual question
feasibility_note: Canonical terms (registry_retrieval): 'original-query lane' = original-query protected lane (lane role 'original' in planned fusion) — already implemented as lane protection; 'obligations' ties to the S5 obligation-aware selection fix for the positional 6-match planner cap (C3), which must exist by S14. Mostly plumbing existing diagnostics into the record.
required_receipt: Sample cell record containing plan, probes[], obligations[], and lanes[] with role='original' marked protected+unmodified.

verbatim: Corpus/document/tree routes and route scores.
action: Add to the run record: corpus routes, document routes (Tier-0 routing card hits), tree-descent routes, and the score attached to each route decision.
where: Tier-0 routing (polymath_doc_summaries projection), tree descent (document_gated_adaptive_tree_descent), route diagnostics; run-record schema
why: route transparency is what makes a wrong answer debuggable to a wrong turn instead of a mystery
feasibility_note: Terminology (registry_retrieval/hierarchy): 'tree' here = summary_tree descent, 'routes' at document level = tier0_routing_card layer — NOT RetrievalTier despite the shared word; the record schema must use distinct keys (retrieval_tier vs routes[]). Tier-0 is ON (librarian plan seam verified live), so route data exists to capture.
required_receipt: Sample cell record with routes[] = {level: corpus|document|tree, target_id, score} for each hop.

verbatim: Vocabulary matches, concept IDs, provenance, and planner expansions.
action: Add to the run record: vocabulary matches with their lexicon identity ids, provenance (which trusted alias method matched), and every planner expansion with its source. Define 'concept ID' in the schema as the corpus_lexicon identity id.
who: backend retriever vocabulary diagnostics + matrix runner
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (_TRUSTED_EXACT_ALIAS_METHODS), corpus_lexicon ids, grounded planner call logs; run-record schema
why: expansion provenance is the audit trail that keeps the vocabulary bridge honest
feasibility_note: TERM COLLISION (registry_alias/registry_latent): 'concept IDs' is ambiguous across four artifacts — corpus_lexicon identities, parent latent_concepts, summary-tree `concepts` (populated on 0/42,990 v2 and 0/1,867 ecom nodes per Audit Delta 2 — recording these would record nothing), and the unbuilt P2.1 concept contract. Schema must pin ONE meaning (lexicon identity id) and name the others separately. Also: 'planner expansions' require the grounded planner to be operational — baseline says model unset → not operational; P1.2 activation is gated behind card.v0+seats+baseline. Until then this field records an honest empty, not a fake.
required_receipt: Sample cell record with vocabulary_matches[] = {lexicon_id, surface, method(provenance)}, planner_expansions[] = {text, source, obligation}; empty-with-reason when planner is off.

verbatim: Candidate counts before/after fusion, reranking, and final selection.
action: Add to the run record: candidate counts at each funnel stage — per-lane pre-fusion, post-fusion (named fusion mechanism), post-rerank, and final selection.
where: planned_fusion.py (fuse_planned_pools), reranker service, final selection; run-record schema
why: funnel counts localize recall loss to the exact stage that dropped the evidence
feasibility_note: Terminology (registry_retrieval, verdict DISTINCT): 'fusion' names several mechanisms — planned_pool_fusion (lane merge), qdrant_rrf, rank_fusion_fallback (the reranker-timeout escape), multi_score_fusion (slated for retirement under the production redesign). The record must name WHICH fusion ran; a rank_fusion_fallback run is a degraded run and must be visible as such (silent-fallback accounting rule).
required_receipt: Sample cell record with candidate_counts = {per_lane:{...}, post_fusion, fusion_kind, post_rerank, final} and rerank_fallback flag when the breaker fired.

verbatim: Corpus/document distribution and reservation reasons.
action: Add to the run record: final-set corpus/document distribution and, for every reserved seat, the reservation reason emitted by the calibrated reservation policy.
where: backend/services/retriever/reservation_policy.py, ranking_policy.py; run-record schema
why: reservation reasons are the receipts that seats were earned by policy, not by floor quota
feasibility_note: Best-substrated field group in the contract: reservation_policy.py (P0.3) is merged and live-probed (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2). Terminology (registry_leftover 'document seats'): qualify seat layer — evidence seat (reservation/ranking_policy) vs document seat (librarian shelf_engine); this field group records the former.
required_receipt: Sample cell record with distribution = {by_corpus, by_doc} and reservations[] = {chunk_id, reason, policy_version}.

verbatim: Answerability, lane coverage, evidence coverage, and refusal reason.
action: Add to the run record: the answerability verdict, recall-lane coverage AND evidence-lane coverage as two separate fields, evidence coverage, and the refusal reason when refusing.
where: services/answerability_tuning.py knobs, chat gate (_build_retrieval_answerability_gate), retriever sufficiency (_evaluate_sufficiency); run-record schema
why: separating 'could not find' from 'chose not to answer' is what makes refusals tunable
feasibility_note: TERM COLLISION flagged by registry_retrieval: 'lane coverage' has TWO real senses — recall_lane_coverage (selection.lane_coverage) and evidence_lane_coverage; the claim's wording lists 'lane coverage, evidence coverage' which maps cleanly IF the schema keeps both names distinct. Answerability substrate is mature (P0.4 7 [x]; twin gates documented: retriever sufficiency = repair driver kept STRICT, chat gate = refusal arbiter).
required_receipt: Sample cell record with {answerability_verdict, recall_lane_coverage, evidence_lane_coverage, evidence_coverage, refusal_reason|null}.

verbatim: Embed, vocabulary, tree, retrieval, graph, rerank, hydrate, and total time.
action: Add to the run record: per-stage wall times — embed, vocabulary, tree, retrieval, graph, rerank, hydrate — plus total, sourced from in-request stage timers (not log scraping).
who: backend per-stage timing instrumentation + matrix runner
where: chat orchestrator / retriever stage timers; overlaps P1.9 'per-stage timing' open item; probe_tier_latency.py precedent; run-record schema
why: stage timings turn 'it was slow' into 'rerank ate 28s', enabling targeted fixes
feasibility_note: Per-stage timing is itself an open P1.9 box (17 open) — this record field and that box should land as one change, not two. The three-tier latency probe already measures totals (Fast 39.8s degraded / Hybrid 14.0s / Graph 18.9s baseline), so the delta is stage-level attribution inside the request.
required_receipt: Sample cell record with timings_ms = {embed, vocabulary, tree, retrieval, graph, rerank, hydrate, total} summing sanely against measured wall time.

verbatim: Selected source IDs, calibrated scores, and evidence text.
action: Add to the run record: final selected source IDs (corpus_id/doc_id/parent/child identity), calibrated reranker scores, and the evidence text actually cited.
where: final evidence packet fields; reranker calibrated scores (torch_fp16 sidecar, sigma((s-mu)/T)); run-record schema
why: the record must let an auditor re-read exactly what the model saw, or 'verified' means nothing
feasibility_note: Substrate exists end-to-end: the final-evidence invariant already requires corpus_id/doc_id/parent-child identity/provenance on evidence, and the :8081 reranker emits calibrated fp16 cross-encoder scores. One caution: run records containing heldout evidence text are eval artifacts — keep them under docs/baselines or backend/evals, never in any persisted retrieval representation, per the eval_firewall contamination rule.
required_receipt: Sample cell record with sources[] = {corpus_id, doc_id, parent_id, child_id, calibrated_score, evidence_text}.

verbatim: A corpus is strict-ready only when:
action: Implement strict_ready as a computed, per-corpus readiness state (the ten gates L1235-L1247) inside the S6 readiness split — a distinct level ABOVE fully_enriched, published via corpus_readiness — rather than leaving it prose.
where: backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py; Mongo corpus_readiness (schema corpus_readiness.v2); S6 readiness-split design
why: a machine-checked strict_ready is what stops 'ready' from meaning whatever the last human believed
feasibility_note: Registry_readiness: 'strict ready' is PLAN-ONLY (canonical strict_ready, zero code symbols today) and the plan already routes it: 'Strict-ready definition ... consumed by readiness split (S6)' (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 1). S6 explicitly ends fully_enriched implying librarian-grade metadata; strict_ready becomes the strong tier alongside metadata_quality_readiness and temporal_capability.
required_receipt: corpus_readiness rows expose strict_ready with per-gate booleans + failure reasons; UGO canary flips gates on/off in a test and the rollup follows.

verbatim: Every eligible document is parsed, structured, indexed, and queryable.
action: Implement gate 1: every eligible document has ingest_stage in the strict set (parsed, structured, indexed) AND is queryable — using the strict fully_enriched stage set, not the weaker QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES.
where: backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py (FULLY_ENRICHED_STAGES :112, per-doc stage rollup); documents.ingest_stage
why: one unparsed document silently missing from retrieval is exactly the failure strict_ready exists to catch
feasibility_note: TERM TRAP flagged by registry_readiness: QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES is deliberately WEAKER than fully_enriched (includes pending-graph/summary variants, ingestion_service.py:39-47) — a strict_ready gate written against 'queryable' would pass documents that fail the claim's own 'parsed, structured, indexed' clause. Gate must be stage==fully_enriched AND queryable, with 'eligible' defined by the L1245 denominator rule.
required_receipt: Gate function + test: corpus with one document at a pending stage reports strict_ready=false naming that doc_id.

verbatim: Qdrant child-vector counts match durable Mongo child counts.
action: Implement gate 2: per-corpus Qdrant child-vector point count equals durable Mongo child-chunk count (per collection, exact), surfaced with both numbers and the delta.
where: per-corpus Qdrant collections (_col_for_corpus) vs Mongo chunks; readiness.py index-projection gates (:368-454 precedent)
why: a Mongo-Qdrant count gap means some children are unsearchable — invisible recall loss
feasibility_note: Cheap deterministic count compare; readiness.py already min-gates fully_enriched on index-projection readiness (lexicon_ready / summary_tree_index_ready / document_profile_index_ready), so this is one more min-gate in an existing pattern. Historical lesson (vector=0 while health stayed green) is precisely why this must be a count parity check, not a health-endpoint check.
required_receipt: Gate emits {mongo_children, qdrant_points, delta:0} per corpus; test with one deleted point flips strict_ready=false.

verbatim: Every summary-required parent has one validated, provider-attributed, child-evidenced summary.
action: Implement gate 3: every summary-required parent has exactly one summary that is (a) schema-validated, (b) provider-attributed (summary_model non-empty), and (c) child-evidenced (child IDs/boundaries/evidence fields verified) — reusing p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify as the gate body.
who: backend readiness service, reusing the P0.1 verify harness
where: backend p0_1_summary_integrity.py; parent_chunks summary fields; summary_jobs
why: unattributed or unevidenced summaries poison every downstream tree route and card
feasibility_note: Strongest live receipt in this section: P0.1 verify PASSES all four corpora today (V — 0 empty-model, 86,880+9,453+1,009+203 all attributed; the checklist's two IN PROGRESS tags are stale per standing rule 3). Gap: the 'child-evidenced' clause maps to P0.1's still-open verify boxes (child IDs/boundaries/evidence field verification) — the gate is only as strong as that verify extension, so land it with this gate.
required_receipt: strict_ready gate wraps `p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify` per corpus, exit 0 required, including the extended child-evidence field checks.

verbatim: Every document summary tree is synchronized.
action: Implement gate 4: per-document summary_tree synchronization check — every document's tree nodes reconcile with current parents (no orphan sections, no missing rollups, passthrough links intact) and the tree's Qdrant index projection matches Mongo.
where: Mongo summary_tree (21,432 sections / 23,394 rollups baseline); summary_tree_index_ready gate (readiness.py); passthrough fields (registry_hierarchy)
why: a desynchronized tree routes descent into sections that no longer exist
feasibility_note: Terminology (registry_hierarchy): 'summary tree' = canonical summary_tree (the RAPTOR tree — same artifact). 'Synchronized' needs a checkable definition; the substrate exists (summary_tree_index_ready already min-gates fully_enriched; registry_readiness lists a distinct 'summary-job reconcile' and 'tier0 projection reconcile' — this gate composes them per document rather than inventing a new mechanism).
required_receipt: Gate emits per-doc tree_sync=true with counts (sections, rollups, passthroughs, orphans:0); test that deletes one tree node flips the gate.

verbatim: Vocabulary projection is complete for the selected extraction contract.
action: Implement gate 5: corpus_lexicon vocabulary projection is complete FOR the corpus's selected extraction contract — every reconciled identity from the active engine's extractions is present in the lexicon and its Qdrant schema payload projection (corpus_{cid8}_schemas) is current-epoch.
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Qdrant corpus_{cid8}_schemas payloads; lexicon_ready gate (readiness.py:368-454); extraction engine selection (Mongo settings endpoints[].enabled)
why: an incomplete lexicon silently disables the vocabulary bridge for exactly the newest content
feasibility_note: lexicon_ready already min-gates fully_enriched — the delta is 'for the selected extraction contract': registry_extraction warns 'ghost_b' names both the stage and one engine, and engine='local' now means a provider LLM, so the gate must read the corpus's ACTIVE engine/contract (schema_version + extractor on ghost_b_extractions) not assume one. Note Audit Delta 2: `extractor` was absent on 0/3,000 sampled rows — that attribution backfill is a prerequisite for this gate to be computable per-contract.
required_receipt: Gate emits {active_contract, identities_expected, lexicon_entries, projection_epoch_current:true}; S5 epoch bump exercises the invalidation path.

verbatim: Required graph extraction is validated and promoted.
action: Implement gate 6: for corpora where graph extraction is required, all durable extractions are schema-validated AND promoted to Neo4j (graph_promotion_jobs complete, zero pending/failed promotions).
who: backend readiness service + graph promotion jobs
where: Mongo ghost_b_extractions (+ ghost_b_failures); backend/services/ingestion/graph_promotion_jobs.py; Neo4j
why: extractions that never promote are dead weight — paid extraction earning zero retrieval value
feasibility_note: Promotion machinery exists (graph_promotion_jobs.py:264 — 'ghost_b_extractions is the durable extraction artifact store', promotion to Neo4j), so the gate is a job-state + validation query. Scope honestly: 'required' must be per-corpus policy (extraction is being re-run for the pair at S11 per the signed disposition matrix), and the known extraction-to-retrieval-metadata gap means promoted-to-Neo4j is the correct bar here — do not stretch this gate to cover the separate promote()-to-payload program.
required_receipt: Gate emits {extractions, validated, promoted, pending:0, failed:0} per corpus with graph required; corpus with one unpromoted row reports strict_ready=false.

verbatim: No durable failed/running/duplicate job remains.
action: Implement gate 7: zero durable jobs in failed or running (stale-lease) state and zero duplicate active jobs for the corpus, across summary_jobs and the other durable job collections.
where: Mongo summary_jobs (baseline: 50,522 succeeded / 500 queued / 775 superseded), job_leases, source_parse_jobs, graph_promotion_jobs
why: a lingering failed/duplicate job means some artifact is stale or double-written under the corpus you are about to trust
feasibility_note: Straight Mongo state query; lease semantics exist (P0.6, lane lease vs cleanup lease distinction per registry_leftover). Baseline shows 500 queued summary jobs — under this gate no corpus with queued work is strict_ready, which is correct strictness, not a gate bug: strict_ready SHOULD be false while durable work is in flight, and the caller waits. Define 'running' violations as lease-expired-stale jobs so healthy in-flight leases are reported but not misclassified as wedged.
required_receipt: Gate emits per-collection {failed:0, stale_running:0, duplicates:0, queued:0} for the corpus; seeded failed job flips the gate in a test.

verbatim: No ontology predicate/entity-type violation remains under the active contract.
action: Implement gate 8: validate all promoted graph edges/entities for the corpus against the ACTIVE ontology contract (allowed predicates, entity types); zero violations remain.
where: Neo4j (promoted graph); ontology contract definition (P2.5 typed signatures territory); validator new
why: ontology violations turn the graph tier into a source of confidently-typed nonsense
feasibility_note: Weakest-substrate gate in the section: there is no machine-readable ontology contract yet (P2.5 typed signatures 0 of 4, P2.4 negation 0 of 5 — both S11), so 'the active contract' does not exist as an artifact to validate against until S11 defines it. Feasible as a gate SHELL at S6 (contract-absent → gate reports not-applicable-with-reason, never silently true), with real enforcement landing at S11. Known floor: ~50% related_to is the correct pure-BERT floor on clean entities — the contract must encode that reality, not wish it away.
required_receipt: Gate emits {contract_id, violations:0} when a contract is active, or {contract_id:null, status:'no_active_contract'} — never a bare pass; post-S11 run on the pair shows real validation counts.

verbatim: Readiness denominators exclude only explicit, documented duplicates or structural skips.
action: Implement the denominator rule: readiness denominators may exclude a document ONLY via an explicit exclusion record (kind: documented-duplicate | structural-skip, with reason + date + actor); anything else counts against strict_ready.
where: backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py denominator computation; documents exclusion fields / dedup records (containment-based doc-dedup pipeline precedent)
why: quietly shrinking denominators is how 100% ready gets faked; explicit exclusions keep the percentage honest
feasibility_note: The dedup program already produces exactly the durable duplicate records this rule needs (containment-based detect/prevent/correct pipeline with duplicate endpoints), so 'documented duplicate' has an artifact to point at. This is the anti-gaming clause of the whole section — it should be reviewed as adversarially as code: any new exclusion kind requires a ledger note.
required_receipt: Readiness output lists exclusions[] = {doc_id, kind, reason, recorded_by, date} and denominator math is reproducible from it; test that an undocumented skip fails the gate.

verbatim: All three retrieval tiers pass smoke and negative-control probes.
action: Implement gate 10: a scripted per-corpus probe suite that runs one smoke query AND one negative-control query on each of the three RetrievalTier values (6 probes) and requires all to pass (smoke answers grounded; negative controls refuse) before strict_ready flips true.
who: backend readiness service invoking the eval harness
where: three_tier_eval.py routes + negative_control probes; wired as the final min-gate in readiness.py; exercised corpus-wide at S14
why: strict_ready must mean 'provably answers and provably refuses' on every tier, not just 'data present'
feasibility_note: All parts exist: three-tier harness enumerates the tiers, negative_control is an established shape, answerability gates fail closed (P0.4). Design decision to make explicit: this gate makes readiness computation run live queries (cost + latency + GPU contention) — run it on readiness RECOMPUTE events, not on every readiness read, and record probe artifacts. S14's full matrix is the corpus-wide superset of this per-corpus gate.
required_receipt: Per-corpus probe artifact: 3 tiers x {smoke: grounded answer, negative_control: refusal with reason} all green, referenced by the strict_ready=true readiness row.
